items.title: Neil the seal swims out to sea, leaving joy in his wake

children.content: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving behind a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

items.title: Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' developer's company in liquidation with unpaid super, millions in debts

children.content: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a round silver stadium jutting into the water, surrounded by green space, with Hobart city behind.

items.title: Aboriginal woman fights trespass charge, cites 'cultural obligation'

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: A 'surge in calls' among pressures mounting on police, review finds

children.content: A review of Tasmania Police finds calls for help have risen by 40,000 annually over the past decade, fuelling demands for investment in early intervention support services.

items.title: Why a UK right-wing leader could soon battle a man dressed as a bin

children.content: Nigel Farage is facing intensifying scrutiny over his finances and millions of dollars' worth of donations. Here's how he got here.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a man dressed as a bin, and another man looking slightly concerned.

items.title: 'Eternity of pain': Angus Taylor issues warning to Australians voting for One Nation

children.content: In his strongest remarks pushing back on One Nation, the opposition leader has accused the right-wing party of being a "column of smoke".

image.altText: Close up of Opposition leader Angus Taylor speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Modi tells Melbourne crowd India aspires to 'grow more, achieve more'

children.content: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

image.altText: Two men, one wearing a gold jacket and the other a suit and tie, hold hands in the air

items.title: Girls are being married off as a climate change 'coping strategy'

children.content: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

items.title: Footage shows interceptor missile test in Australian outback

children.content: The prototype system included a mix of Australian and American technology, with Defence aiming to build a medium-range system to defend against missiles or aircraft bound for Australia.

items.title: When politics becomes content the risks multiply

children.content: Every press conference, reel and podcast is now a chance for politicians to connect with voters. Yet the strategy that broadens an MP's reach can also amplify their biggest mistakes.

items.title: At least 28 killed in shoe factory fire in China, state media says

children.content: More than 180 firefighters battled the deadly blaze at a factory in China's 'shoe capital'.

image.altText: Dozens of firefighters aim hoses at a smouldering and badly burnt building

items.title: World leaders gifted vintage revolvers by Erdogan after NATO summit

children.content: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogansaid the gifts were intended as a showcase of his nation's defence industry.

image.altText: a brown paper bag, with an open giftbox inside, with a silver and wooden revolver in it.

items.title: Beyoncé-approved: How Hiatus Kayote earned 15 years of A-list respect

children.content: They've made admirers out of music's biggest names over their 15-year career, but this Australian group are always thinking about the future.

items.title: From giant crab men to searing satire, here's the best TV of 2026 so far

items.title: Friday news quiz: Red cards, ballistic missiles and Pam the Bird

items.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury

children.content: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

gallery.altText: Gout Gout competing at the Oslo Diamond League meet.

items.title: As the ceasefire cracks, Iran gets 'a complete reset' of pressure on its oil trade

children.content: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

image.altText: A gas flare on an oil production platform in the Soroush oil fields is seen alongside an Iranian flag in the Persian Gulf, Iran

items.title: Family desperate for information on missing Queensland mother

children.content: Police are searching for a 30-year-old woman who was last seen in Toowoomba on Tuesday.

image.altText: A woman smiles at the camera

items.title: 'Pretty cool': Trump announces win for Ukraine amid fiery NATO meeting

children.content: Donald Trump says his country will probably continue to attack Iran and says Ukraine will be given permission to manufacture much-needed Patriot missiles as it fights Russia's full-scale invasion.

image.altText: Two men in suits sitting and speaking.

items.title: 'Bad mistake': ABC's editorial director questioned over inaccurate report

children.content: The ABC and SBS were called to give evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, with the inquiry hearing that a number of complaints had been received about the public broadcasters' coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: The key ingredient keeping the snow season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

items.title: Three corrections officers stood down as review launched into prisoner escape

children.content: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

image.altText: A close up of a prison guard's badge

items.title: Children's commissioner quits as CLP set to pass controversial welfare changes

children.content: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: Court told former Wagga Wagga MP was 'kept in dark' over visa scheme

children.content: Daryl Maguire's barrister has told a jury it was "unbelievable" to think the ex NSW MP would recruit people in his electorate to take part in an allegedly fraudulent visa scheme.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket, white shirt and tie leaves a building

items.title: Streets reopen around New York high-rise after buckled beams stabilised

children.content: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

items.title: Water in the air an 'untapped water resource' until now

children.content: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

items.title: Swimming Australia fights 'scary' fake news in world-first initiative

children.content: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: Jake answers the call and saddles up for ride of a lifetime

children.content: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.
